In LTL, 'only after' can be formalized by requiring that before sending the control-traffic message, both queries must have occurred at some point prior. This global constraint must hold for every instance of the process (DM_i). The property is thus: globally, it's always the case that if a controlTraffic message is sent from Local Node to DM_i, then previously the Local Node has queried the MPR Selector Set and the Duplicate Set.
